title: Heatwave grips Rajasthan, Maharashtra above, temprature reaches 44°C: Storms and heavy rain in UP, Bihar, K... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
- Storms & Rain Claim Lives In UP, Bihar, Karnataka | Heatwave Grips Rajasthan, Maharashtra
Mercury stays above 44°C in Rajasthan,Maharashtra:Storms, rain kill 32 in UP, Bihar, and Karnataka
After nearly two weeks of intense heat, large parts of India witnessed some relief on Thursday as thunderstorms, strong winds, rain, and hailstorms swept across several regions, bringing temperatures down by 3–5°C.
States including Uttar Pradesh, Delhi, Bihar and Jharkhand experienced a drop in temperatures following the weather activity.
However, heatwave conditions persisted in parts of the country. Chandrapur recorded the highest temperature at 44.6°C, while Barmer and Jaisalmer also reported temperatures above 44°C. Severe heatwave conditions were observed in Amaravati and Rentachintala.
Meanwhile, the sudden weather changes turned deadly in several regions. At least 17 people were killed in thunderstorms in Uttar Pradesh, while five deaths were reported in Bihar. In Bengaluru, the death toll from heavy rain-related incidents rose to 10.
In a separate incident, an 11-year-old boy died after being struck by lightning at his home in Kathua.
Rain, storms and alerts
In contrast to the heat, several regions have been placed under heavy rainfall alerts. An orange warning has been issued for states including Odisha, Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, sub-Himalayan West Bengal, and southern Karnataka.
The IMD has also warned of strong winds reaching speeds of 50–60 kmph, along with lightning and possible hailstorms in affected areas.
Death toll rises
Severe weather events on Thursday led to multiple casualties across the country. In Bengaluru, heavy rainfall-related incidents claimed 10 lives. In Jammu and Kashmir’s Kathua district, an 11-year-old child died after lightning struck a house in Bani tehsil.
In Uttar Pradesh, 13 people lost their lives due to storms and rain, with Sultanpur reporting seven deaths. Bihar also reported five fatalities linked to the extreme weather
A person trying to find relief by drinking water to escape the scorching heat in Bikaner, Rajasthan, on Thursday
In Agra, Uttar Pradesh, the temperature dropped after a storm and rain. Tourists arrived in large numbers to see the Taj Mahal
Even in New Delhi, dark clouds gathered in the afternoon. A drop in temperature was observed due to strong winds and rain.
A view of dark clouds above the Brahmaputra River in Guwahati, Assam, where rainfall has been continuous for the past week
Heavy rain occurred in Ooty, Nilgiris, Tamil Nadu. Passengers passing on the road amidst the rain. The weather here will remain humid on Friday
Weather forecast for coming days
May 2:
Heatwave likely in western Rajasthan bordering Pakistan
Dust storms and rain alerts across Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal and South Karnataka
Heavy to very heavy rainfall expected in parts of the Northeast and southern states
May 3:
Rain expected in Jammu & Kashmir, Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand and several central and eastern states
Orange alert for heavy rain in parts of eastern India
Heatwave conditions to persist in western Rajasthan
Humid weather likely in Tamil Nadu and Puducherry
